description: Scaler fitted on full dataset including test set — classic data leakage
symptom: Model achieves 95% val accuracy but only 72% on held-out test
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Customer Churn Prediction

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: PostgreSQL database
  Records: 10,000 customer records
  Features: 15 numerical, 5 categorical
  Target: churn (binary)
  Status: OK — all records loaded successfully

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: mean imputation for numerical, mode for categorical
  StandardScaler fitted on FULL dataset (train + val + test)
  Encoding: OneHotEncoder for categorical features
  Status: Complete

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Features created: tenure_months * monthly_charges = total_value
  Feature selection: top 15 by mutual information
  Status: OK

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: random 70/15/15 split
  Stratified by target: yes
  Status: OK

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: GradientBoosting(n_estimators=200, max_depth=5)
  Training time: 45 seconds
  Status: Complete

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Val Accuracy: 95.2% | Val F1: 0.94
  Test Accuracy: 72.1% | Test F1: 0.68
  Metric: accuracy
  Status: Large gap between val and test performance

preprocessing.description: StandardScaler fitted on full dataset including test set, leaking test distribution into training
preprocessing.fix: fit StandardScaler on training data only, then transform val and test sets

evaluation.description: Using accuracy metric for potentially imbalanced churn prediction
evaluation.fix: use F1 score or AUC-ROC as primary metric for imbalanced classification

description: Time series split done randomly instead of temporally — destroys ordering
symptom: Model performs well on validation but fails on future data
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Stock Price Direction Prediction

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: Yahoo Finance API
  Records: 5,000 daily records (2005-2024)
  Features: OHLCV + 12 technical indicators
  Target: next_day_direction (up/down)
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: forward fill for price data
  Normalization: MinMaxScaler per feature
  Status: OK

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Created features: RSI_14, MACD, Bollinger_Bands, SMA_20, SMA_50, EMA_12
  Rolling window: 20 days
  Status: OK

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: random 80/20 shuffle split
  shuffle=True applied before split
  Stratified by target: yes
  Status: Complete — split looks balanced

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: LSTM(hidden=128, layers=2, dropout=0.2)
  Epochs: 50 | Batch size: 64
  Status: Converged at epoch 35

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Val Accuracy: 68.2% | Val F1: 0.67
  Live trading accuracy (Jan 2024): 49.8%
  Status: Model fails in production

train_val_split.description: Random shuffle split on time-series data causes future data to leak into training set
train_val_split.fix: use temporal split — train on data before cutoff date, validate on data after

feature_engineering.description: Rolling features computed before split can include future values near boundaries
feature_engineering.fix: compute rolling features independently on train and val sets after splitting

description: Mean imputation on skewed data and evaluation on training set
symptom: Model shows 99% accuracy but fails on new data
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Insurance Fraud Detection

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: CSV files from claims system
  Records: 50,000 insurance claims
  Features: 25 mixed-type features
  Target: is_fraud (binary, 2% positive)
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: mean imputation for claim_amount (highly right-skewed, mean=$45,000, median=$2,500)
  Null handling: mean imputation for age (normally distributed)
  Encoding: LabelEncoder for all categorical features
  Status: Complete

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Features: claim_to_income_ratio, claim_frequency, days_since_last_claim
  Scaling: StandardScaler on all features
  Status: OK

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: stratified 80/20 split
  Status: OK

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: RandomForest(n_estimators=500, max_depth=None)
  Training time: 120 seconds
  Status: Complete

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Evaluated on: TRAINING SET (not validation set)
  Train Accuracy: 99.8% | Train F1: 0.99 | Train AUC: 0.999
  Status: Metrics look excellent

preprocessing.description: Mean imputation on highly right-skewed claim_amount ($45k mean vs $2.5k median) introduces bias
preprocessing.fix: use median imputation for skewed features like claim_amount

evaluation.description: Model evaluated on training set instead of held-out validation set, giving inflated metrics
evaluation.fix: evaluate on held-out validation/test set, not the training data

description: Label encoding for nominal categories and class imbalance ignored
symptom: Model accuracy is 96% but fraud recall is near zero
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Credit Card Fraud Detection

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: Transaction database
  Records: 284,807 transactions
  Features: V1-V28 (PCA), Amount, Time
  Target: is_fraud (binary, 0.17% positive — 492 fraud cases)
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: none needed (no nulls)
  Scaling: StandardScaler on Amount and Time
  Status: OK

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Encoding: LabelEncoder for merchant_category (electronics=0, groceries=1, travel=2, dining=3, gas=4)
  Note: merchant_category is NOMINAL (no natural ordering)
  New features: hour_of_day, day_of_week from timestamp
  Status: Complete

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: stratified 80/20 split
  No class balancing applied
  Status: OK

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: LogisticRegression(C=1.0, max_iter=1000)
  No class_weight parameter set
  Training time: 8 seconds
  Status: Converged

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Accuracy: 99.83% | Precision: 0.91 | Recall: 0.02 | F1: 0.04
  Confusion: TP=10, FP=1, FN=482, TN=284314
  Status: Model predicts almost everything as non-fraud

feature_engineering.description: LabelEncoder applied to nominal merchant_category — imposes false ordinal relationship
feature_engineering.fix: use one-hot encoding for nominal categorical features like merchant_category

model_training.description: No class balancing strategy for 99.83%/0.17% class split — model learns to predict majority class
model_training.fix: use class_weight='balanced' or oversample minority class with SMOTE

description: Target leakage through feature and wrong scaler for bounded data
symptom: Suspiciously high val performance that doesn't generalize
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Loan Default Prediction

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: Banking database
  Records: 30,000 loan applications
  Features: income, loan_amount, credit_score, employment_years, debt_to_income, payment_history_score, default_probability_estimate
  Target: defaulted (binary)
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: median imputation
  Outliers: no handling
  Status: OK

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Scaling: StandardScaler on ALL features including probability features (bounded 0-1)
  Features used: income, loan_amount, credit_score, employment_years, debt_to_income, payment_history_score, default_probability_estimate
  Note: default_probability_estimate was computed AFTER knowing if the loan defaulted
  Status: Complete

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: random 80/20 split, stratified
  Status: OK

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: XGBoost(n_estimators=300, max_depth=6, learning_rate=0.1)
  Status: Converged

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Val AUC: 0.997 | Val F1: 0.96
  Feature importance: default_probability_estimate = 0.72 (dominates)
  Status: Suspiciously high performance driven by one feature

feature_engineering.description: default_probability_estimate is derived from the target variable — including it leaks future information
feature_engineering.fix: remove default_probability_estimate from features — it was computed using the target label

preprocessing.description: StandardScaler on bounded probability features (0-1) distorts their meaning
preprocessing.fix: use MinMaxScaler for bounded features like probability scores and ratios

description: Duplicates not removed causing leakage, and preprocessing fit on full data
symptom: Val metrics unexpectedly close to train metrics — model seems too good
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Patient Readmission Prediction

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: Hospital EHR database
  Records: 20,000 patient records
  Duplicates: 3,400 exact duplicate rows (17%) due to data export bug
  Features: 30 clinical features
  Target: readmitted_30days (binary)
  Status: Loaded successfully (duplicates not flagged)

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: KNN imputation (k=5)
  KNN imputer fitted on FULL dataset (before split)
  Encoding: OneHotEncoder for diagnosis codes
  Status: Complete

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Features: length_of_stay, num_procedures, num_medications, comorbidity_index
  Scaling: RobustScaler
  Status: OK

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: random 80/20 split
  Note: Duplicate rows can appear in BOTH train and val sets
  Status: Complete

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: GradientBoosting(n_estimators=150, max_depth=4)
  Status: Converged

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Train Accuracy: 89.2% | Val Accuracy: 88.5%
  Test Accuracy (external): 71.3%
  Status: Small train-val gap but large generalization gap

data_ingestion.description: 3,400 duplicate rows (17%) leak across train/val split, inflating validation metrics
data_ingestion.fix: remove duplicate rows before any splitting to prevent data leakage

preprocessing.description: KNN imputer fitted on full dataset including val/test data, leaking statistical information
preprocessing.fix: fit KNN imputer on training data only, transform val and test separately

description: Outliers not handled and accuracy used for imbalanced dataset
symptom: High accuracy but poor performance on minority class
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Network Intrusion Detection

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: Network traffic logs
  Records: 100,000 connection records
  Features: duration, src_bytes, dst_bytes, count, srv_count, and 35 more
  Target: attack_type (normal=97%, attack=3%)
  Outliers: duration column has values up to 58,329 (mean=47, median=0)
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: none (no nulls)
  Outlier handling: NONE — extreme values left as-is
  Encoding: OneHotEncoder for protocol_type, service, flag
  Status: Complete (no outlier treatment)

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Feature selection: variance threshold (remove constant features)
  Scaling: StandardScaler (heavily affected by outliers)
  Status: OK

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: stratified 80/20 split
  Status: OK

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: SVM(kernel='rbf', C=1.0)
  Training time: 300 seconds
  Status: Converged

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Accuracy: 97.2%
  Attack class: Precision=0.45, Recall=0.12, F1=0.19
  Normal class: Precision=0.98, Recall=0.99, F1=0.99
  Metric used: accuracy (not appropriate for 97/3 split)
  Status: High accuracy masks poor attack detection

preprocessing.description: Extreme outliers in duration (max=58329, median=0) distort StandardScaler and model training
preprocessing.fix: clip outliers using IQR method or remove extreme values before scaling

evaluation.description: Accuracy metric for 97/3 class split gives misleading 97.2% — model barely detects attacks
evaluation.fix: use F1 score, AUC-ROC, or precision-recall as primary metric for imbalanced classification

description: Shuffle before split on time series and fit_on_test for encoder
symptom: Model shows good val metrics but fails on new monthly data
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Monthly Revenue Forecasting

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: Sales database
  Records: 36,000 daily sales records (2014-2024)
  Features: date, store_id, product_category, units_sold, price, promotions, weather
  Target: monthly_revenue
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: forward fill
  Categorical encoding: OrdinalEncoder fitted on FULL dataset
  Status: Complete

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Created: month, quarter, year, rolling_3month_avg, yoy_growth
  Lag features: revenue_lag_1, revenue_lag_3, revenue_lag_12
  Status: OK

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Data shuffled before split: YES (np.random.shuffle applied)
  Strategy: 80/20 split after shuffling
  Status: Complete

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: LightGBM(n_estimators=500, learning_rate=0.05)
  Status: Converged at boosting round 312

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Val RMSE: $1,234 | Val MAPE: 3.2%
  Jan 2025 prediction error: $15,678 (MAPE: 42.1%)
  Status: Severe performance drop on future data

train_val_split.description: Random shuffling of time-series data destroys temporal ordering and creates future leakage
train_val_split.fix: do not shuffle time series data — use chronological split with training before validation period

preprocessing.description: OrdinalEncoder fitted on full dataset including future data, leaking category distributions
preprocessing.fix: fit encoder on training data only, handle unseen categories in val/test with fallback

description: Wrong aggregation in features and wrong scaler for sparse data
symptom: Feature importance shows unexpected patterns, predictions are off
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Customer Lifetime Value Prediction

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: E-commerce transaction history
  Records: 500,000 transactions from 50,000 customers
  Features: customer_id, transaction_date, amount, category, quantity
  Target: lifetime_value_next_year
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Aggregation per customer: 
    total_transactions = MEAN(transaction_count)  [should be SUM]
    total_spend = MEAN(amount)  [should be SUM]
    avg_basket_size = MEAN(quantity)  [correct]
  Null handling: zero fill
  Status: Complete

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Encoding: OneHotEncoder for top 20 categories (creates sparse matrix)
  Scaling: StandardScaler on ALL features including sparse one-hot columns
  Note: 80% of feature values are exactly 0.0 (sparse after one-hot)
  Status: Complete

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: random 80/20 split
  Status: OK

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: Ridge(alpha=1.0)
  Status: Converged

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Val RMSE: $890 | Val R2: 0.34
  Expected R2 for this data: > 0.60
  Feature importance: total_transactions has near-zero importance (unexpected)
  Status: Underperforming expectations

preprocessing.description: total_transactions and total_spend use MEAN instead of SUM — they represent per-transaction averages, not totals
preprocessing.fix: use SUM aggregation for total_transactions and total_spend to capture actual customer value

feature_engineering.description: StandardScaler on sparse one-hot encoded features destroys sparsity and distorts feature distances
feature_engineering.fix: use MinMaxScaler or no scaling for sparse features, or scale numerical and categorical features separately

description: Off-by-one in sequence window and evaluation on train data
symptom: Model shows near-perfect metrics that seem too good to be true
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Energy Consumption Forecasting

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: Smart meter readings
  Records: 365,000 hourly readings (1 year)
  Features: temperature, humidity, hour, day_of_week, is_holiday, consumption
  Target: consumption_next_hour
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: linear interpolation
  Scaling: MinMaxScaler
  Status: OK

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Sequence window: 24 hours lookback
  Window construction: features[t-24:t+1] -> target[t+1]
  Note: window includes t+0 which IS the target hour's features
  This means: consumption at time t is included in features predicting consumption at t+1
  But consumption[t] is ALSO the target shifted by 1
  Status: Complete

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: temporal split (first 80% train, last 20% val)
  Status: OK

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: Transformer(d_model=64, nheads=4, layers=2)
  Epochs: 30 | LR: 0.001
  Status: Converged

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Evaluated on: training set (not validation set)
  Train RMSE: 0.02 kWh | Train R2: 0.999
  Status: Metrics appear perfect (suspiciously so)

feature_engineering.description: Sequence window [t-24:t+1] includes features at time t, which contains near-identical information to target at t+1
feature_engineering.fix: use window [t-24:t] to exclude the current timestep, preventing trivial prediction from autocorrelation

evaluation.description: Metrics computed on training data, not the held-out temporal validation set
evaluation.fix: evaluate on the temporal validation set (last 20%) to assess true generalization

description: Target leakage through derived feature and missing value strategy
symptom: One feature has 0.95 correlation with target — model oversimplified
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Employee Attrition Prediction

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: HR database
  Records: 15,000 employee records
  Features: age, salary, department, performance_score, tenure, satisfaction_survey, exit_interview_score
  Target: left_company (binary)
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: mean imputation for satisfaction_survey (30% missing — mostly from employees who left)
  Note: satisfaction_survey is missing BECAUSE employees left — missingness is informative
  Encoding: OneHotEncoder for department
  Status: Complete

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Features used: all original + exit_interview_score
  Note: exit_interview_score is only available AFTER an employee has left
  Correlation with target: 0.95
  Status: Complete

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: stratified 80/20 split
  Status: OK

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: RandomForest(n_estimators=100)
  Status: Converged

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Val AUC: 0.98 | Val F1: 0.95
  Feature importance: exit_interview_score = 0.60, satisfaction_survey = 0.15
  Status: Model dominated by leaked features

feature_engineering.description: exit_interview_score is only available after an employee leaves — using it leaks the target
feature_engineering.fix: remove exit_interview_score from features — it's a post-event variable that leaks the target

preprocessing.description: Mean imputation for satisfaction_survey ignores that missingness is informative (missing = left company)
preprocessing.fix: create a is_survey_missing binary feature instead of mean imputation, or use median and add missing indicator

description: Fit-transform leak in preprocessing and wrong split for grouped data
symptom: Model performance drops 15% between cross-validation and production
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Medical Diagnosis Prediction

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: Hospital medical records
  Records: 25,000 lab results from 5,000 patients (avg 5 visits each)
  Features: blood_pressure, glucose, cholesterol, BMI, age, smoking, family_history
  Target: diabetes_diagnosis (binary)
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  PowerTransformer (Yeo-Johnson) fitted on ALL data (train + val + test)
  Null handling: median imputation
  Status: Complete

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  BMI categories: underweight, normal, overweight, obese
  Interaction features: glucose * BMI, age * cholesterol
  Status: OK

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: random 80/20 split at ROW level
  Note: Same patient can appear in BOTH train and val sets (multiple visits)
  No grouping by patient_id
  Status: Complete

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: XGBoost(n_estimators=200, max_depth=4)
  Status: Converged

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Val AUC: 0.92 | Val F1: 0.87
  New patient AUC: 0.77 | New patient F1: 0.71
  Status: Significant drop on truly unseen patients

preprocessing.description: PowerTransformer fitted on all data including test set, leaking distribution information
preprocessing.fix: fit PowerTransformer on training data only, then transform validation and test sets

train_val_split.description: Random row-level split allows same patient in train and val — patient-level leakage
train_val_split.fix: use GroupKFold or GroupShuffleSplit with patient_id as the group to ensure patient-level separation

description: Class imbalance ignored and StandardScaler on probability features
symptom: 99.5% accuracy but the model is useless for detecting rare events
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Rare Disease Screening

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: National health survey
  Records: 200,000 patient screening results
  Features: 40 biomarkers, demographics, family history
  Target: has_rare_disease (binary, 0.5% positive — 1,000 cases)
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: median imputation
  Scaling: StandardScaler on biomarker_probability_scores (bounded 0-1)
  Status: Complete

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  PCA: reduced 40 biomarkers to 10 components
  New features: age_group, BMI_category
  Status: OK

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: stratified 80/20 split
  No class balancing applied
  Status: OK

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000)
  No class_weight parameter
  No oversampling/undersampling
  Status: Converged

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Accuracy: 99.5% | Recall: 0.03 | F1: 0.06
  Model detects only 6 out of 200 disease cases in validation
  Status: Model predicts 'healthy' for nearly all samples

model_training.description: No class balancing for 99.5%/0.5% split — model learns to always predict majority class
model_training.fix: use class_weight='balanced', or apply SMOTE oversampling, or use cost-sensitive learning

preprocessing.description: StandardScaler on probability scores (bounded 0-1) shifts mean and can produce negative values
preprocessing.fix: use MinMaxScaler for bounded probability features to preserve their 0-1 range semantics

description: Wrong aggregation for count features and scaler fitted after split but on train+val
symptom: Feature values don't match expected domain ranges
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Ad Click-Through Rate Prediction

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: Ad serving logs
  Records: 1,000,000 ad impressions
  Features: user_id, ad_id, position, device, hour, day_of_week, historical_clicks
  Target: clicked (binary)
  Status: OK

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  User-level aggregation:
    user_total_clicks = MEAN(click_count)  [should be SUM — we need total clicks, not average]
    user_total_impressions = MEAN(impression_count)  [should be SUM]
    user_ctr = user_total_clicks / user_total_impressions  [correct formula but wrong inputs]
  Status: Complete

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Encoding: OneHotEncoder for device, TargetEncoder for ad_id
  Scaling: RobustScaler fitted on train + validation combined
  Status: Complete

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: temporal split (chronological)
  Status: OK

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: LightGBM(n_estimators=100, learning_rate=0.1)
  Status: Converged

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Val AUC: 0.71 | Val LogLoss: 0.42
  Expected AUC for this data: > 0.78
  user_total_clicks feature importance: near zero (unexpected)
  Status: Underperforming

preprocessing.description: User click and impression counts use MEAN instead of SUM aggregation — loses magnitude information
preprocessing.fix: use SUM aggregation for user_total_clicks and user_total_impressions to capture actual counts

feature_engineering.description: RobustScaler fitted on train + validation combined, leaking validation statistics into training
feature_engineering.fix: fit scaler on training data only, then transform validation data separately

description: Duplicate rows and ordinal encoding for nominal data
symptom: Model confidence is uncalibrated, predictions cluster around certain values
artifact:
ML Pipeline: Restaurant Rating Prediction

Stage 1 - data_ingestion:
  Source: Restaurant review dataset
  Records: 80,000 reviews (12,000 are exact duplicates from web scraping)
  Duplicates: 15% of data is duplicated
  Features: cuisine_type, price_range, location, service_score, food_score, ambiance_score
  Target: overall_rating (1-5 stars)
  Status: OK (duplicates not detected)

Stage 2 - preprocessing:
  Null handling: mode imputation
  Status: OK

Stage 3 - feature_engineering:
  Encoding: OrdinalEncoder for cuisine_type (italian=0, chinese=1, indian=2, mexican=3, japanese=4, french=5, thai=6)
  Note: cuisine_type is NOMINAL — no natural ordering between cuisines
  Scaling: StandardScaler on numerical features
  Status: Complete

Stage 4 - train_val_split:
  Strategy: random 80/20 split
  Note: Same duplicate reviews can appear in both train and val
  Status: Complete

Stage 5 - model_training:
  Model: GradientBoosting(n_estimators=200)
  Status: Converged

Stage 6 - evaluation:
  Val RMSE: 0.31 | Val R2: 0.89
  External test RMSE: 0.52 | External test R2: 0.74
  Status: Overly optimistic validation metrics

data_ingestion.description: 12,000 duplicate reviews (15%) leak across train/val split, inflating validation metrics
data_ingestion.fix: remove duplicate rows before splitting to prevent data leakage across train/val sets

feature_engineering.description: OrdinalEncoder imposes false ordering on nominal cuisine_type (italian < chinese < indian has no meaning)
feature_engineering.fix: use one-hot encoding for nominal categorical features like cuisine_type
